task_completion.visual_evidence: The Rancher dashboard is visible in the browser at https://localhost, indicating successful login.
The Rancher UI shows the Workloads, Deployments, or Services view specifically filtered for the 'ecommerce' namespace.
The 'api-gateway' deployment in the Rancher UI shows a green 'Active' status, indicating the broken image tag was fixed and the pod is running.
The 'web-frontend' service or deployment in the Rancher UI shows a green 'Active' status and/or the service shows active endpoints, indicating the selector mismatch was resolved.
The 'cache-layer' deployment in the Rancher UI shows a green 'Active' status, indicating the ConfigMap port was corrected and the pod is running.
The 'batch-processor' deployment in the Rancher UI shows a green 'Active' status, indicating the excessive memory request was reduced and the pod is no longer Pending.

integrity.description: Agent used the Rancher web interface
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the Rancher web UI in a browser, rather than just using a terminal with kubectl commands.

integrity.description: Agent genuinely interacted with the resources
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent navigating through Rancher menus, editing YAML or form fields in the UI, and applying changes.

integrity.description: Results are from the actual application
integrity.visual_evidence: The healthy states should be visible within the standard Rancher interface, not in a text editor, local HTML file, or console output.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires fixing 4 specific failures in the 'ecommerce' namespace via the Rancher UI: 1) 'api-gateway' has a broken image tag ('nginx:broken-tag-xyz-nonexistent') causing ImagePullBackOff. 2) 'web-frontend' has a service selector mismatch (selector 'app: web-frontend' vs pod label 'app: frontend-app'). 3) 'cache-layer' has a ConfigMap with the wrong Redis port (6380 instead of 6379). 4) 'batch-processor' has an excessive memory request (32Gi) causing a Pending state. Success is achieved when all 4 workloads show a green 'Active' state in Rancher.
